DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS MARK CASEY AND CARRIE CASEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL IRVING ZARETSKY TO RESPOND TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS, AND ASSOCIATED REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. QUESTION 1 - PLAINTIFF WAIVED THE OBJECTION TO THE QUESTION BY FAILING TO OBJECT. QUESTION 2-6 -THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGE IS WAIVED AS TO THE MATTERS PERTAINING TO PATRICK BUSCOVICH'S FILING OF AN ADMINISTRATIVE COMPLAINT AND TESTIMONY AT AN ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. PARTIES ARE TO AGREE ON AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; NOT REPORTED

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-28-2011 IN 206, PLAINTIFF IRVING ZARETSKY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT VACATED THE TRIAL DATE OF 1/17/12 AND PUT THE CASE BACK IN CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF 2/17/12 AT 2:00PM IN DEPT. 610. THERE IS A SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING INSPECTION HEARING AND ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS WITH THIS CASE. NOTICE OF THE CMC TO BE MAILED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: KATHERINE FEINSTEIN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Irving Zaretsky To Respond To Deposition Questions, And Associated Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARK CASEY AND CARRIE CASEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL IRVING ZARETSKY TO RESPOND TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS, AND ASSOCIATED REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS.
Appearances: Robert DeVries of the Law Offices of Robert DeVries (546.5100) for plaintiff and responding party; James Biernat of the Biernat Law Group (650.802.0410) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: granted. Question 1 - Plaintiff waived the objection to the question by failing to object. Question 2-6 -The attorney work product privilege is waived as to the matters pertaining to Patrick Buscovich's filing of an administrative complaint and testimony at an administrative hearing. The court denied the request for sanctions. Parties are to agree on and submit a proposed form of order. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla Miller; Not reported

Ntc Of Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF IRVING ZARETSKY'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER.
Appearances: Robert DeVries of the Law Offices of Robert DeVries (546.5100) for plaintiff and moving party; James Biernat of the Biernat Law Group (650.802.0410) for defendants and responding parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. The attorney work product privilege is waived as to the matters covered in Patrick Buscovich's testimony at the administrative hearing. Parties to agree on and submit a proposed form of order. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla Miller; Not reported
